summary: Validated backend APIs and frontend critical flows for SES Asamblea voting MVP using public preview URL. End-to-end flow (admin login -> upload padrón -> create/open point -> delegate register/login -> single vote -> live/public/directiva results) works.

integration_issues.issue: Recharts logs warnings (width/height = -1) during route transition/render timing.

action_items: Move JWT SECRET_KEY and default admin credentials to environment variables; avoid hardcoded secrets in backend/server.py.
Remove or protect /api/public/setup in non-development environments because it publicly exposes admin credentials.
Stabilize live chart container sizing to avoid Recharts negative dimension warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py hardcodes SECRET_KEY and default admin credentials (security risk).
backend/server.py exposes admin username/password via /api/public/setup without auth (critical in production).
seed_data_creation: Created test delegate (document prefix TEST/UI), one test agenda point, and one test vote in preview environment.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite is ready at /app/backend/tests/test_voting_mvp_api.py and passed (8/8). UI e2e validated on public URL with admin and delegate workflows. Existing test artifacts include UI screenshots in /app/test_reports and JUnit XML in /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml.
rca of the issue: Primary findings are code/security level, not flow breakage: credentials are intentionally exposed by implementation (public setup endpoint + hardcoded constants), and live chart warning appears due container sizing timing when chart mounts after route changes.
